nodes.label:
Compelling
Narrative
nodes.title: Bold claims about transformative technology create a compelling story that attracts attention and investment
nodes.example: Quantum computing will break all encryption, cure diseases, and revolutionize finance — a narrative so sweeping it resists scrutiny.

nodes.label:
Media
Coverage
nodes.title: Press amplifies innovation claims, prioritizing dramatic narratives over technical nuance
nodes.example: Headlines like 'Quantum Supremacy Achieved!' generate clicks, while the fine print — that the demo solved no useful problem — gets buried.

nodes.label:
Investor
Interest
nodes.title: Capital allocators respond to compelling narratives and media attention by exploring investment opportunities
nodes.example: VCs see quantum computing coverage everywhere and begin allocating funds, worried about missing the next big platform shift.

nodes.label:
Valuation
Inflation
nodes.title: Capital inflow drives up company valuations far beyond what current technology or revenue justifies
nodes.example: QC startups with zero revenue reach billion-dollar valuations based on potential alone, while classical computing competitors trade at 10x earnings.

nodes.label:
Talent
Rush
nodes.title: High valuations and media attention attract researchers and engineers, draining talent from other productive fields
nodes.example: Top physics PhDs leave productive classical research to join QC startups offering equity packages based on inflated valuations.

nodes.label:
Over-
promising
nodes.title: Pressure from investors and inflated expectations drives companies to make claims beyond what the technology can deliver
nodes.example: Companies promise 'quantum advantage within 2 years' to justify valuations, knowing the physics doesn't support that timeline.

nodes.label:
Delivery
Failures
nodes.title: When overpromised milestones are missed, credibility erodes and the gap between claims and reality becomes visible
nodes.example: Promised fault-tolerant systems don't materialize. Error rates remain orders of magnitude too high. Customers see no ROI.

nodes.label:
Disillusion-
ment
nodes.title: Accumulated failures and unmet promises trigger a reassessment of the entire technology narrative
nodes.example: After years of missed milestones, institutional investors begin writing down QC positions and media coverage turns skeptical.

nodes.label:
FOMO
Pressure
nodes.title: Fear of missing out creates urgency to invest before 'it's too late,' bypassing careful due diligence
nodes.example: Fund managers invest in QC not because of technical conviction, but because competitors are investing and LPs are asking about it.

nodes.label:
Information
Asymmetry
nodes.title: Most investors lack the physics knowledge to evaluate QC claims, creating a knowledge gap exploited by hype
nodes.example: Investors can't distinguish between 1,000 noisy qubits and 1,000 error-corrected logical qubits — a difference that changes everything.

nodes.label:
Skeptic
Voices
nodes.title: Critical analysts and scientists who question the narrative provide a balancing force — when they are heard
nodes.example: Physicists who point out that error correction requires 1,000-10,000 physical qubits per logical qubit are dismissed as 'not visionary.'

nodes.label:
Reality
Gap
nodes.title: The widening distance between what is promised and what is technically achievable
nodes.example: Roadmaps promise millions of logical qubits by 2030, but labs struggle to maintain coherence in systems of 100 physical qubits.

edges.label:
(+) generates
coverage
edges.title: Compelling narratives are irresistible to media — they generate clicks and views

edges.label:
(+) attracts
investors
edges.title: Widespread media coverage signals opportunity, drawing investor attention

edges.label:
(+) inflates
valuations
edges.title: More capital chasing limited QC companies drives valuations up

edges.label:
(+) attracts
talent
edges.title: High valuations fund generous compensation packages that draw top talent

edges.label:
(+) pressure
to deliver
edges.title: Talented hires need to justify their salaries and equity with ambitious promises

edges.label:
(+) leads to
failures
edges.title: Promises that exceed technical reality inevitably result in missed milestones

edges.label:
(+) causes
disillusionment
edges.title: Visible delivery failures erode confidence and trigger reassessment

edges.label:
(-) dampens
investment
edges.title: Disillusionment reduces investor willingness to fund QC ventures (balancing loop B1)

edges.label:
(+) pressure to
overpromise
edges.title: Sky-high valuations create pressure to justify them with ever-bolder claims

edges.label:
(-) reality
check
edges.title: Disillusionment forces more realistic framing of innovation claims (balancing loop B2)

edges.label:
(+) rising values
fuel FOMO
edges.title: Watching valuations climb makes non-investors fear they're missing out

edges.label:
(+) FOMO drives
investment
edges.title: Fear of missing out bypasses due diligence and drives capital in (reinforcing loop R1)

edges.label:
(+) ignorance
enables hype
edges.title: When investors can't evaluate claims, they rely on narratives instead of evidence

edges.label:
(-) skeptics
dismissed
edges.title: Information asymmetry means skeptics' technical arguments fall on deaf ears

edges.label:
(-) caution
slows investment
edges.title: When heard, skeptic voices reduce investor enthusiasm (balancing loop B3)

edges.label:
(+) gap feeds
skepticism
edges.title: As the gap between promises and reality widens, skeptics gain evidence and credibility

edges.label:
(+) gap drives
disillusionment
edges.title: A visible reality gap accelerates the shift from enthusiasm to disillusionment

edges.label:
(+) failures
widen gap
edges.title: Each delivery failure makes the distance between claims and reality more obvious

R1.label: R1: FOMO Loop
R1.description: Rising valuations create FOMO, which drives more investment, which inflates valuations further.

B1.label: B1: Disillusionment Loop
B1.description: The main bubble cycle: investment leads to overpromising, failures, and disillusionment that dampens further investment.

B2.label: B2: Reality Check Loop
B2.description: Disillusionment forces more realistic narratives, slowing the hype cycle at its source.

B3.label: B3: Skeptic Loop
B3.description: Skeptic voices provide a balancing force against investor enthusiasm, when information asymmetry allows them to be heard.
